title: SessionEvent
description:
Envelope around one event as it travels the wire or sits in the log.

`seq` is assigned by the session store (monotonic per session, gapless for
persisted events); adapters and transports never mint sequence numbers.

AgentCaps.description:
Everything an `Agent` implementation declares about itself.

Merged from static profile defaults and startup probes; flows to clients
verbatim via the `Hello` handshake and transport initialization.

emits_structured_events.description: False for agents that emit only plain markdown (no structured events).

reasoning_visible.description: Whether reasoning/thinking output is visible in the stream.

oneOf.description:
Emitted once per session start: which implementation is serving it,
what it can do, and how it was selected.

resolution_trace.description: Human-readable trace of how this agent/provider was resolved.

oneOf.description: A fragment of markdown text appended to the open message.
A fragment of reasoning text.

Full-text snapshot superseding all earlier text of the message.
Emitted by snapshot-only sources instead of deltas.
A fragment of a tool call's JSON arguments, as the model streams them.
Progress note from a running tool.

The full [`ToolCall`] record, re-emitted on every status transition.
The log keeps the history; the transcript keeps the latest.
The agent's working plan changed (task list tools, ACP plan updates).
The agent asked the user structured questions (`AskUserQuestion`).
A slash command was resolved into this turn's prompt.

Live relay of a child event into the parent stream (ephemeral — the
child persists its own log).

A live subscriber lagged and missed events; re-sync from the store
starting at `from_seq`. Never persisted.

An event this build doesn't know. Preserved verbatim — never dropped,
never a crash.

selected.description: Selected option labels (one unless `multi_select`), or free text.

AttachmentCaps.description: Which attachment kinds a prompt may carry.

BlobSource.description:
Where binary data (images, files) comes from.

Transports may send small payloads inline (`Base64`), reference remote
content (`Url`), or point at local files (`Path`). The engine resolves
`Path` sources (read + base64, size-capped) before content reaches a
provider, so provider clients only ever see `Base64` or `Url`.

CancelSupport.description: How a turn can be interrupted.

oneOf.description: Clean interrupt; the session survives.
Only by killing the backing process.

CommandInfo.description: One available slash command, declared so clients can render autocomplete.

CommandSource.description: Where a slash command comes from.

oneOf.description: Shipped with the engine (template or engine-control command).
From the user's config directory.
From the project's config directory.
Native to a delegated agent, passed through.

summary_markdown.description:
Markdown summary that replaces the compacted prefix when building
model context.

oneOf.description: CommonMark text. The only text kind in the canonical model.
Image input/output (png/jpeg/webp/gif).
File attachment as prompt input (pdf, documents, arbitrary files).

Model reasoning. `signature` preserves provider-verifiable blobs
(e.g. Anthropic thinking signatures) so they round-trip on resume.
The model requested a tool invocation.
A tool result being fed back to the model.

Provider-specific block that must round-trip verbatim (e.g. Anthropic
`redacted_thinking`). Keyed by provider; the owning provider re-encodes
it, everyone else treats it as inert.

EngineError.description:
A normalized failure. The `message` must be actionable: what failed, why,
and what to do about it.

detail.description: Source-specific payload for debugging; never required to handle.

retry_after_ms.description: Suggested wait before retrying (from rate-limit headers etc.).

retryable.description: Whether retrying the same request may succeed.

oneOf.description: No credentials configured for the selected provider/agent.
Credentials present but rejected.
A delegated agent's process died unexpectedly.
The remote side violated its own wire protocol.
Required external CLI is not installed.

HookOutcomeKind.description: What a hook did (as recorded in the event stream).

HookPoint.description: Where in the loop a hook fires.

McpPassthrough.description: How MCP server configuration reaches a delegated agent.

oneOf.description: Via a launch flag (e.g. `--mcp-config`).
Via the protocol's session setup (e.g. ACP `session/new.mcpServers`).

MessageId.description: Identifies one message (user or assistant) within a session.

ModeInfo.description: An operating mode a delegated agent exposes (e.g. build/plan).

ModelDiscovery.description: How an agent's model list is discovered.

oneOf.description: A fixed list known up front.
Probed at runtime (e.g. opencode's `/config/providers`).
The agent owns model selection; the engine cannot choose.

ModelRef.description:
Reference to a model, optionally provider-qualified:
`"anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5"` or a bare `"claude-sonnet-4-5"`.

PermissionCaps.description: Permission-related capabilities of an agent implementation.

interactive.description: Can surface live permission round-trips (vs static policy only).

PermissionDecision.description: A user's answer to a permission request.

oneOf.description: Allow and persist a rule so this is never asked again.

PermissionDecisionKind.description: The options a client may present for a pending permission request.

PermissionMode.description: How the engine treats tool calls that would normally ask the user.

oneOf.description: Ask on anything not covered by a rule.
Auto-allow file edits (the `Fs` category); ask for the rest.
Deny all mutating tools; read-only research still runs.
Never ask: deny anything that would prompt.
Allow everything. Requires an explicit opt-in flag on the runner.

PlanEntry.description: One entry of the agent's working plan (task list).

oneOf.description: A native provider client.
A delegated external agent.

stderr_tail.description: Last lines of the process's stderr, for diagnosis.

oneOf.description: The engine itself.

ProviderId.description:
Identifies an LLM provider implementation (`"anthropic"`, `"openai"`, ...).

Not a UUID — a stable, human-chosen key used in configuration, in
[`crate::content::ContentBlock::Opaque`] round-tripping, and in
provider-namespaced request passthrough.

Question.description: A structured question the agent asks the user (`AskUserQuestion` tool).

allow_custom.description: When true the user may type a free-text answer instead of picking options.

QuestionOption.description: One option of a multiple-choice question.

ResumeSupport.description: Whether and how a session can be resumed.

oneOf.description: The backing agent resumes its own session natively.
Resumed by replaying seed history into a fresh session.

SessionId.description: Identifies one session: an append-only event log owned by one agent.

SessionMeta.description:
Descriptor of one session (the append-only event log it names is stored
separately by a `SessionStore`).

agent_id.description:
Which agent implementation owns this session
(`"native"`, `"claude-code"`, ...).

parent_id.description: Set for subagent sessions; links the session tree.

provider_session_id.description: The backing agent's own session id, for native resume.

StreamingGranularity.description: Granularity of the event stream an agent emits.

oneOf.description: Token-level deltas.
Whole messages as they complete.
Full-text snapshots that supersede earlier ones.

TokenUsage.description: Token accounting for one model call or aggregated over a turn.

ToolCall.description: The durable, inspectable record of one tool invocation.

message_id.description: The assistant message that requested this call.

read_only.description:
Read-only calls may execute concurrently; mutating calls run
sequentially. Also feeds the permission policy.

ToolCallId.description: Identifies one tool invocation across its whole lifecycle.

oneOf.description: Requested by the model through native tool use.
Injected by a hook.
Issued on behalf of a subagent.
Observed from an external (delegated) agent.

ToolCallStatus.description:
Lifecycle state of a tool call.

```text
Pending ──► AwaitingPermission ──► Running ──► Completed
   │                │                 │  └───► Failed
   │                └───► Denied      └──────► Cancelled
   └─────► Running | Denied | Cancelled
```

oneOf.description: Parsed from model output, queued for execution.
Waiting on a user permission decision.
Executing.

Finished. The result may still carry `is_error: true` (a *tool-level*
error fed back to the model, e.g. "file not found").
Engine-level failure: panic, timeout, I/O error in the tool runtime.
Refused by the permission policy, a hook, or the user.
The turn was interrupted while this call was pending or running.

ToolCallTiming.description: Wall-clock milestones of one call. All fields are unix epoch milliseconds.

finished_at_ms.description: When a terminal state was reached.

permission_wait_ms.description: Time spent waiting for a permission decision, if any.

is_error.description:
Tool-level error: fed back to the model as a failed result, never a
loop failure.

structured.description: Machine-readable payload for consumers that don't want to parse text.

oneOf.description: Structured output for tools that return machine-readable data.

TurnId.description: Identifies one turn: one user prompt and everything until the loop idles.

TurnStopReason.description: Why a whole turn ended (loop level).

oneOf.description: The model finished without requesting more tools.
The loop hit its per-turn iteration bound.
